Sollich 3a

When i got here this morning, the conveyor speed of the capper was set to 60hz. Centerline is 45. In my opinion, this is likely the cause of the over feeding issue. The drive screw speed and other timing is based on 45hz not 60. We have to get out of the mindset that faster means higher output. When it causes issues and downtime, faster is worse. If we truly NEED to go faster, then we need to make comprehensive changes to every setting. Just speeding up the belt inside a machine that has a dozen moving parts is counter productive.

Centerline

The FFS continues to see overweight bags being kicked off. At least 75+ an hour. I think it has to do with the hopper and buckets. Bucket number 1 has an abnormal vibration patterns in the rest of the area. Bucket 12nkeeps overfilling and won't empty which is causing overspill. 


 


The over weight bags are almost twice the qty per the spec. Normal is 17-19 and we are seeing bags that are in the high 30s and low 40s.


 


I have requested maintenance take a look and they had suggested starting with a power cycle but am waiting for them to be done with ML3.

At the beginning of the shift we noticed some caramel accumulation on the Tunnel 2 roller. (See pictures below)



We continued observing how much accumulation was being added. (See pictures below)









By 8:45PM, the accumulation buildup is enough that we’ll be pausing for 30min at 10PM. 

During this time:
The Enrober team will clean and clear accumulated caramel on and around Tunnel 2 drive roller. 

The pack team will clean and clear the accumulated salt on and around the Tunnel 3 drive roller, as well as under the Tunnel 3 belts. 
Dakota Adair Curt Willis

The caramel buildup on the rollers was more than anticipated. 

So we were able to clean the rollers, but not reassemble them before the end of our shift


Joseph Scott Dakota Adair Curt Willis
My team said Last Saturday during EOW clean there was NOT caramel like this accumulated in the tunnel 2 drive Curt Willis
We will assemble the drive and get running
Our QA team has concerns the belt of tunnel 2 may have touched the floor before it was re assembled at the drive. We will stop the line and empty the almonds and clean the belt

Hi Team,
We are experiencing an issue with ingredient mixing in the mixing tank. It appears that the ingredients are not blending properly, and I believe this may be due to the current 1,000 lb batch size.
I would like to suggest the following actions:
1. Reduce the batch size to 714 lb (using 50 lb sugar bags).
2. Add the butter as the last ingredient, as we do in the other Sollich Kitchens.
Italu, please review this recipe for your approval.
Please let me know your thoughts.
Thank you. Justin Garner Katya Farnsworth Curt Willis Italu Martinez

Hi Italu Martinez
We cooked the first batch at 222°F, and the viscosity came out to 22,300 cP. Our target is 28,000–35,000 cP.

Could you please let me know what cooking temperature you would recommend for the next batch?
Justin Garner Katya Farnsworth
Batch # 1 Viscosity, AW, Brix
Has a 3rd batch been made?
No, we are waiting for your suggestions
It seems like the viscosity was going up
Yes, but still low.
Italu Martinez, do you want us to cook another batch at 222? Or change the cook temperature and cook another batch?
Ideally we would make changes after the 3rd batch
The viscosity increased because we also increased the cooking temperature from 220°F to 222°F.
Thanks for that info
I suggest increasing the cooking temperature to 224°F.
We’ll do whatever you recommend.
I would only go up a degree for now. Seems like you guys are very close and we only have 1 data point at 222.
Sounds good.  We will cook 1 more batch at 223 and wait for results.
Italu Martinez, did you see Marcos post from this morning about ingredients mixing?
Batch # 2 Viscosity, AW, Brix
I did not until now
What do you think about making a smaller recipe to improve the mixing?
I agree
Please review this and let me know if we can use it.
Yes you can use it
Waiting for viscosity test for 3rd batch that was cooked at 223 Justin Garner
The viscosity was within range at 28,600 cP. Now we will cook another batch, but with a batch size of 714 lb. Italu Martinez Justin Garner Katya Farnsworth
The mixing tank improved by 100%
Marcos Jimenez, there is a glare on the board covering the tolerance. Can you take another picture please.
Ok
The viscosity of the third batch was cooked to 223. The viscosity turned out to be excellent. 30700

Hi Italu Martinez
I started cooking the 714 lb batch, and we got a viscosity of 34,900 cP. The maximum target is 35,000 cP.
Maybe because this is a smaller batch size, the temperature has a greater effect on the viscosity.
Please let me know what temperature you recommend for the next 714 lb batch.
Thank you.
Justin Garner Katya Farnsworth
Please go down 1.5 degrees
Italu Martinez, we ended up with a very low viscosity. Please let me know what temperature we should set for the next batch. Justin Garner Katya Farnsworth

Was only 1 batch cooked?
2 batch

Italu Martinez
The viscosity is 28,200 cP. Would you like to increase the cooking temperature?

Ok 👍

Italu Martinez,
